title: Oral and Dental Health Program Learning Outcomes
content:
Oral and Dental Health Program Learning Outcomes

Program Learning Outcomes

1. Possesses fundamental theoretical and practical knowledge in the field of oral and dental health; utilizes this knowledge effectively in clinical processes and patient services.

2. Acts in accordance with professional ethical rules, laws, regulations, and legislation; is aware of individual rights and responsibilities.

3. Uses basic computer programs, information, and communication technologies related to the field effectively; demonstrates proficiency in digital systems during professional practices.

4. Can carry out assigned tasks independently by using core knowledge and skills, and takes an active role within a team when necessary.

5. Evaluates acquired knowledge and skills with a critical approach; analyzes professional problems and develops solution proposals.

6. Recognizes the importance of lifelong learning; follows scientific and technological developments and prioritizes continuous professional development.

7. Demonstrates sensitivity to social events and current developments; informs relevant individuals and institutions regarding their field-specific expertise; contributes to social responsibility projects.

8. Operates in compliance with quality management processes; possesses adequate awareness of occupational health and safety, individual and public health, and environmental protection.

9. Contributes to the collection, application, and evaluation of field-related data in accordance with scientific, cultural, and ethical values.

10. Follows current information and innovations in the field using a foreign language and communicates effectively with colleagues.
